folio.label: 148v
folio.l: A thyngö , that also god my soule saue
In al this world , there is no creature
That ete or dronke hatú , of this confiture
Nat but the mountaunce , of a corn~ of whete
That he ne shal , his lyf , a non for lete
ña sterue he shal , and that in lasse while
Than thow wolt gon a pase , nat but amyle
The poysou~ is so strongö , and violent
This cursed man , hatú in his hand I hent
This poysou~ in a box , and sithe he ran
In to the next strete , vn to a man
And borwed hi~ , large botelles thre
And in the two , hise poysou~ poured he
The thridde he kept clene , for his drynke
For al the nygút , he shop~ hi~ for to swynke
In cariyngö of the gold , out of that place
And whan this Riotour , witú sory grace
Had filled witú wyn , hise grete botelles thre
To hise felawes , a geyn repairetú he
What nedetú it , to Sermone of it more
For rigút as they had cast , his detú bifore
Rigút so they han hiö slayn , and that a noon
And whan that this was don , thus spak that oon
Now late vs sitte and drynke , and make vs merie
And afterward , we wyln his body berie
And witú that word , it happed hiö ¹ caas
To take the botel , there the poysou~ was
And drank , and ©af his felawe drynke also
For whicú a noon , they storuen bothe two
But certes I suppose , that Auycen
Wroot neuäe in no canouö , ne in no fen
Moo wonder signes , of empoysonyngö
Than hadde theise wrecches two , or here endyngö
Thus ended ben , theise homycydes twoo
And eke the false empoysonere , also
O cursed synne , of alle cursednesse
O . traytours homycyde , O wykkednesse
O glotonye , luxurie , and hasardye
Thow blasfemour , of crist , witú vilenye
And othes grete , of vsage and of pride
Allas mankynde , how may it betyde
That to thy creature , whicú that the wrougúte
And witú his päcious hert blod , the bougúte
